---
title: "Material Balances"
author: "DOFPro group"
---
{{< include /LinkKey.qmd >}}

# Introduction

Material balances are applications of the **General Balance Equation** to **mass** or **moles**. They are among the most common calculations in chemical engineering and process design.

Material balances are used to determine stream compositions, flow rates, recycle and purge requirements, reactor conversions, and separation performance. In simple systems they may only require bookkeeping. In more complex systems they become the foundation for reactor modeling, phase-equilibrium calculations, and process design.

This section begins with the general balance framework and then moves through the most common kinds of systems in which material balances are applied.

## [The General Balance Equation](../general/index.qmd)

The videos in the **General** section introduce systems, control volumes, and the balance framework used throughout the rest of this section. If you are new to balances, start there.

## [Mixers, Dividers, and Separators](MixDivideSeparateVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

The simplest material balances are around nonreactive unit operations such as mixers, dividers, and separators. These provide the basic bookkeeping tools used throughout process engineering.

**Gotta Keep ‘Em Separated**<br/>*Mixer, Divider, and Separator mole balances*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/pRg1m0_9Psg), [Info Page](MixDivideSeparateVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](40-UnitOpsMixDivSepVisuals.qmd)

## Chemical Reactors

Chemical reactors require material balances that account for chemical reaction. Depending on the problem, the reaction may be described in terms of **extent of reaction**, **fractional conversion**, **kinetics**, or **equilibrium**.

### [Fractional Conversion Reactors](FractionalConversionReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

These videos introduce reactor balances written in terms of **extent of reaction**, **fractional conversion**, **yield**, and **selectivity**.

**When You Saw It, What Was the Extent of Your Reaction? Part 1**<br/>*Chemical Reactor Single Reaction, $\xi$ and $f_{\mathrm{A}}$*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/HKYYp5UxwpA), [Info Page](FractionalConversionReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](41-UnitOpsReactPart1Visuals.qmd)

**When You Saw It, What Was the Extent of Your Reaction? Part 2**<br/>*Chemical Reactor Multiple Reactions with Example, $\xi$ and $f_{\mathrm{A}}$*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/XmLXR_KOZMk), [Info Page](FractionalConversionReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](42-UnitOpsReactPart2Visuals.qmd)

**When You Saw It, What Was the Extent of Your Reaction? Part 3**<br/>*Chemical Reactor Yield and Selectivity*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/PPi-ka3iRvI), [Info Page](FractionalConversionReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](42.5-UnitOpsReactPart3Visuals.qmd)

### [Kinetic Reactors](KineticReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

These videos introduce reactor balances for systems in which the reaction rate is determined by **kinetics**, including batch reactors, CSTRs, and PFRs.

**How Did You Think I Would React?**<br/>*Basic Kinetic Reactor Mole Balance*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/_qxHszVZYGk), [Info Page](KineticReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](46-KineticsIntroJTFVisuals.qmd)

**Wishing Upon a CSTR**<br/>*Batch, CSTR, PFR*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/6tnVFq7dSbE), [Info Page](KineticReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](47-BatchCSTRPFRIntroVisuals.qmd)

**It’s Totally Tubular, Man!**<br/>*CSTR, PFR, Batch Example*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/OAzXx9Kq2nU), [Info Page](KineticReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](47.5-ReactorExamplesVisuals.qmd)

### [Equilibrium Conversion Reactors](EquilibriumReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

These videos treat reactors in which the reaction is assumed to proceed to **equilibrium** rather than being limited by kinetics.

**React But Maintain Your Equilibrium Part 1**<br/>*Basics of equilibrium reactors*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/0GS8XEHRBMk), [Info Page](EquilibriumReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](54-EquilReact1Visuals.qmd)

**React But Maintain Your Equilibrium Part 2**<br/>*Equilibrium reactor example*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/GI8tuSRrIcc), [Info Page](EquilibriumReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](55-EquilReact2Visuals.qmd)

### [Combustion Reactions](CombustionReactionsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

Combustion balances are a particularly important class of reacting-system material balances because they combine stoichiometry, gas compositions, excess air, and flue-gas analysis.

**Flame On, Torch!**<br/>*Combustion reactions*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/G2sKW_6N2BA), [Info Page](CombustionReactionsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](59-CombRxnsPart1Visuals.qmd)

**A Combustible Mixture!**<br/>*Sample combustion reactions*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/hvIk-evMp88), [Info Page](CombustionReactionsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](60-CombRxnsPart2Visuals.qmd)

## [Process Flowsheets](RecyclingBypassVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

Real processes often contain **recycle**, **bypass**, and **purge** streams. These make the bookkeeping more interesting and more realistic.

**Recycling Before Recycling Was Cool**<br/>*Basics of recycle*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/R7tIQyBUWn8), [Info Page](RecyclingBypassVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](43-RecycleBypassPartVisuals.qmd)

**Leaving Radiator Springs In the Dust**<br/>*Basics of bypass and a recycle example*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/tjzDu7_n-NI), [Info Page](RecyclingBypassVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](44-RecycleBypassPart2Visuals.qmd)

**The Symbolic Meaning of Recycling**<br/>*Analytic solution of a recycle PFD*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/CF18jpqZzgc), [Info Page](RecyclingBypassVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](45-RecycleSymbSolVisuals.qmd)

## Properties of Liquids and Gases

Material-balance calculations often require relationships among **mass, moles, volume, pressure, temperature, and composition**. These video sets introduce the most common property models used in later calculations.

### [Liquid Properties](LiquidMixturesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

**Alcohol and Water DO Mix!**<br/>*Liquid and Solid Density Calculations*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/_tjYMjWR4fE), [Info Page](LiquidMixturesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](61-LiqSolDenJTFVisuals.qmd)

### [Gas Properties](IdealAndRealGasesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

These videos introduce ideal-gas and real-gas models, along with mixture rules used in process calculations.

**Ideal Gases EXPOSED: Are They Even Real?!!**<br/>*Basics of $P V = n R T$*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/L-DEw6dGRps), [Info PageIdealAndRealGasesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](62-IdealGasVisuals.qmd)

**When Getting Gassed Isn’t Ideal, Part 1**<br/>*Real gas intro*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/1sU4_YdcKr4), [Info Page](IdealAndRealGasesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](63-Visuals.qmd)

**When Getting Gassed Isn’t Ideal, Part 2**<br/>*Real gas example*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/Q6ZK_Ou967o), [Info Page](IdealAndRealGasesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](64-Visuals.qmd)

**REAL Gas Mixtures EXPLAINED – Don’t Let This Cost You Points on Your Exam! Part 1**<br/>*Explanation of Kay's Rule and DWSIM for real gas mixtures*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/yI3jj1jCSAM), [Info Page](IdealAndRealGasesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](65-Visuals.qmd)

**REAL Gas Mixtures EXPLAINED – Don’t Let This Cost You Points on Your Exam! Part 2**<br/>*Example of Kay's Rule and DWSIM for real gas mixtures*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/Pbizi39c1Rk), [Info Page](IdealAndRealGasesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](66-Visuals.qmd)

## Multiphase Equilibria

Material balances become especially interesting when multiple phases are present. These video sets introduce phase diagrams, dew and bubble calculations, flash calculations, and equilibrium stage models.

### [Reading Phase Diagrams](ReadingPhaseDiagramsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

**Can You Tell an Azeotrope from a Peritectic? Get Your Phase Diagrams Right! Part 1**<br/>*Phase Diagrams, Gibbs Phase Rule*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/NUS4Y_pnWZk), [Info Page](ReadingPhaseDiagramsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](70-IntroMultiphasePart1Visuals.qmd)

**Can You Tell an Azeotrope from a Peritectic? Get Your Phase Diagrams Right! Part 2**<br/>*Ternary Phase Diagrams, Lever Rule*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/QBREikNSvGI), [Info Page](ReadingPhaseDiagramsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](71-IntroMultiphasePart2Visuals.qmd)

### [Bubble, Dew, and Flash Calculations](RaoultsLawDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

**Show Some Flash and Dew the Dew with a Little Bubbly!**<br/>*Graphical BUBL P, DEW P, BUBL T, DEW T, and FLASH calculations*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/shKvpcsJIEM), [Info Page](GraphicalDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](72-BubbleDewFlashVisuals.qmd)

**Oh, Henry!**<br/>*Gas-Liquid Equilibrium with one condensible component*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/dCOQy1C7yf0), [Info Page](HenrysLawVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](73-Visuals.qmd)

**Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash? Part 1**<br/>*Raoult's law, BUBL P, DEW P*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/vSkSRi6HYVQ), [Info Page](RaoultsLawDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](74-Visuals.qmd)

**Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash? Part 2**<br/>*BUBL T, DEW T*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/9GyrfJzg-tg), [Info Page](RaoultsLawDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](75-Visuals.qmd)

**Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash? Part 3**<br/>*FLASH*, *Pxy*, *Txy*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/bG15qQ9gB4c), [Info Page](RaoultsLawDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](75.5-Visuals.qmd)

**Using DWSIM to Create Binary Phase Diagrams**<br/>*DWSIM, Binary Phase Diagrams*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/5NUchIg8q7E), [Info Page](DWSIMBinaryPhaseVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

### [Equilibrium Separation Stages](EqullibriumStagesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)

These videos apply material balances together with phase-equilibrium relationships to model staged separations such as distillation and liquid-liquid extraction.

**The Shocking Truth About Equilibrium Stages—Are You Doing It Wrong? Part 1**<br/>*Equilibrium Stage Equations*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/bY9fl5DJzmk), [Info Page](EqullibriumStagesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](85-EquilibriumSeparationStages1Visuals.qmd)

**The Shocking Truth About Equilibrium Stages—Are You Doing It Wrong? Part 2**<br/>*Distillation Examples: Binary Phase Diagram, Raoult's law*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/wc6gv7lNtI0), [Info Page](EqullibriumStagesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](86-EquilibriumSeparationStages2Visuals.qmd)

**The Shocking Truth About Equilibrium Stages—Are You Doing It Wrong? Part 3**<br/>*Liquid-Liquid Extraction Example*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/jcChVoELvFo), [Info Page](EqullibriumStagesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](86.5-EquilibriumSeparationStages3Visuals.qmd)
